headline: AI economics and pricing pressure led the week as system design broke out and a wave of low-level tooling concepts entered.
body: AI economics and API both held leading positions while cost and pricing dominated the discussion, with a TechCrunch piece warning of token price increases as major labs approach public markets and a charts-driven post weighing capital expenditure against hypothetical returns. System design broke out on the technique side, tracking OpenAI's continued work on a super app and a claim that chat as the dominant interface is ending. A cluster of new tooling and method entrants appeared, including cost measurement, training-free methods, content addressing, lattice heap, categorical types, and MLIR. On the weakening side, benchmark, evals, and software engineering evaluation cooled even as engineers voiced career anxiety about LLM-assisted development and bootstrapped founders measured a different ROI from AI coding.

cited_items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
Billions spent and hypothetical returns: the AI boom explained with six charts
OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
